commonMistakes: Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.
Confusing React Server Components with server-side rendering.
Assuming cached data is fresh without an explicit policy and invalidation path.
Placing security-sensitive validation only in the client or middleware layer.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Explain static rendering using a new example and justify the result.
Explain dynamic rendering using a new example and justify the result.
Explain partial pre-rendering using a new example and justify the result.
Explain choosing a strategy using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Static rendering** → **Dynamic rendering** → **Partial pre-rendering** → **Choosing a strategy**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: Static rendering is best understood as follows: Static rendering produces reusable output ahead of requests and suits data that does not depend on request-specific information.
choices.label: Static rendering produces reusable output ahead of requests and suits data that does not depend on request-specific information.

choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
choices.label: Use Static, Dynamic, and Partial Pre-Rendering when it makes intent, ownership, or data flow clearer; avoid adding it mechanically when a simpler, equally explicit solution is sufficient.

sections.body: Static rendering produces reusable output ahead of requests and suits data that does not depend on request-specific information.
sections.title: Static rendering

sections.body: Dynamic rendering produces output at request time for request-specific, personalized, or uncached data.
sections.title: Dynamic rendering

sections.body: Partial prerendering combines a static shell with dynamic regions behind Suspense boundaries. APIs must be version-pinned because the model evolves.
sections.title: Partial pre-rendering

sections.body: Choose rendering per region based on freshness, personalization, latency, cacheability, and cost.
sections.title: Choosing a strategy

sections.explanation: Static and request-time regions can coexist behind a boundary.
sections.title: Worked example

summary: Static, Dynamic, and Partial Pre-Rendering connects Static rendering, Dynamic rendering, Partial pre-rendering.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Static, Dynamic, and Partial Pre-Rendering

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: Learners frequently use static, dynamic, and partial pre-rendering without a clear model of why it behaves as it does; this lesson makes that model explicit. It belongs to the **Streaming, Rendering Strategies, and Caching** module.
